testcase.name: test_get_env_vars
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "token", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "2022-01-01", "END_DATE": "2022-12-31", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when all environment variables are set correctly. """
( organization, repository_list, gh_app_id, gh_app_installation_id, gh_app_private_key, gh_app_enterprise_only, token, ghe, start_date, end_date, sponsor_info, link_to_profile, > ) = env.get_env_vars() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env.py:68: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ env.py:112: in get_env_vars gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
env_var_name = 'GH_APP_ID'
def get_int_env_var(env_var_name: str) -> int | None: """Get an integer environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as an integer or None. """ > env_var = env.int(env_var_name, None) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:34: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_auth_with_github_app_installation_missing_inputs
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_auth_with_github_app_installation_missing_inputs>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "my_organization", "GH_APP_ID": "12345", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_auth_with_github_app_installation_missing_inputs(self): """Test that an error is raised there are missing inputs for the gh app""" with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as context_manager: > env.get_env_vars()
test_env.py:218: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ env.py:112: in get_env_vars gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def get_int_env_var(env_var_name: str) -> int | None: """Get an integer environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as an integer or None. """ > env_var = env.int(env_var_name, None) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:34: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_invalid_start_date
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_invalid_start_date>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "token", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "2022/01/01", "END_DATE": "2022-12-31", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_invalid_start_date(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when invalid start date given. Expect a ValueError to be raised. """
with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm: > env.get_env_vars()
test_env.py:135: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ env.py:112: in get_env_vars gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def get_int_env_var(env_var_name: str) -> int | None: """Get an integer environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as an integer or None. """ > env_var = env.int(env_var_name, None) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:34: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_missing_values
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_missing_values>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "2022-01-01", "END_DATE": "2022-12-31", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_missing_values(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when none of the environment variables are set. Expect a ValueError to be raised. """
with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm: > env.get_env_vars()
test_env.py:107: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ env.py:112: in get_env_vars gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def get_int_env_var(env_var_name: str) -> int | None: """Get an integer environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as an integer or None. """ > env_var = env.int(env_var_name, None) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:34: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_no_dates
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_no_dates>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "token", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "", "END_DATE": "", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_no_dates(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when all environment variables are set correctly and start_date and end_date are not set. """
( organization, repository_list, gh_app_id, gh_app_installation_id, gh_app_private_key, gh_app_enterprise_only, token, ghe, start_date, end_date, sponsor_info, link_to_profile, > ) = env.get_env_vars() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

env_var_name = 'GH_APP_ID'
def get_int_env_var(env_var_name: str) -> int | None: """Get an integer environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as an integer or None. """ > env_var = env.int(env_var_name, None) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:34: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_bool_env_var_that_does_not_exist_and_default_value_returns_false
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env_get_bool.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_bool_env_var_that_does_not_exist_and_default_value_returns_false>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "TEST_BOOL": "true", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_bool_env_var_that_does_not_exist_and_default_value_returns_false(self): """Test that gets a boolean environment variable that does not exist and default value returns: false """ > result = get_bool_env_var("DOES_NOT_EXIST", False) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env_get_bool.py:76: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
env_var_name = 'DOES_NOT_EXIST', default = False
def get_bool_env_var(env_var_name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool: """Get a boolean environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. default: The default value to return if the environment variable is not set.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as a boolean. """ > return env.bool(env_var_name, default) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:22: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_bool_env_var_that_does_not_exist_and_default_value_returns_true
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env_get_bool.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_bool_env_var_that_does_not_exist_and_default_value_returns_true>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "TEST_BOOL": "false", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_bool_env_var_that_does_not_exist_and_default_value_returns_true(self): """Test that gets a boolean environment variable that does not exist and default value returns: true """ > result = get_bool_env_var("DOES_NOT_EXIST", True) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env_get_bool.py:62: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
env_var_name = 'DOES_NOT_EXIST', default = True
def get_bool_env_var(env_var_name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool: """Get a boolean environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. default: The default value to return if the environment variable is not set.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as a boolean. """ > return env.bool(env_var_name, default) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:22: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_false
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env_get_bool.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_false>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "TEST_BOOL": "false", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_false(self): """Test that gets a boolean environment variable that exists and is false""" > result = get_bool_env_var("TEST_BOOL", False) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env_get_bool.py:34: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
env_var_name = 'TEST_BOOL', default = False
def get_bool_env_var(env_var_name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool: """Get a boolean environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. default: The default value to return if the environment variable is not set.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as a boolean. """ > return env.bool(env_var_name, default) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:22: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_false_due_to_invalid_value
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env_get_bool.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_false_due_to_invalid_value>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "TEST_BOOL": "nope", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_false_due_to_invalid_value(self): """Test that gets a boolean environment variable that exists and is false due to an invalid value """ > result = get_bool_env_var("TEST_BOOL", False) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env_get_bool.py:48: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
env_var_name = 'TEST_BOOL', default = False
def get_bool_env_var(env_var_name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool: """Get a boolean environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. default: The default value to return if the environment variable is not set.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as a boolean. """ > return env.bool(env_var_name, default) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:22: NameError

testcase.name: test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_true
testcase.failure:
self = <test_env_get_bool.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_true>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "TEST_BOOL": "true", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_true(self): """Test that gets a boolean environment variable that exists and is true""" > result = get_bool_env_var("TEST_BOOL", False) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env_get_bool.py:22: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
env_var_name = 'TEST_BOOL', default = False
def get_bool_env_var(env_var_name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool: """Get a boolean environment variable.
Args: env_var_name: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. default: The default value to return if the environment variable is not set.
Returns: The value of the environment variable as a boolean. """ > return env.bool(env_var_name, default) ^^^ E NameError: name 'env' is not defined
env.py:22: NameError
